name: AI Upscaler Plugin
overview: AI-Powered Video Upscaling Plugin with Docker Microservice Architecture
description: Revolutionary AI upscaling plugin with Docker-based AI processing. Supports NVIDIA CUDA/TensorRT, Intel OpenVINO, AMD ROCm, and CPU. Features automated hardware benchmarking, intelligent fallback system, pre-processing cache, sidebar integration, camera-style video filters with live preview, and AI face restoration (GFPGAN / CodeFormer).
changelog: v1.8.3.9: Hotfix from the FIRST LIVE RUN of the v1.8.3.8 importer/converter on a real server. (1) ZIP imports (AnimeJaNai series) failed with an sha256 mismatch: OpenModelDB pins the INNER .onnx file, not the zip container - and one AnimeJaNai release zip ships FIVE model variants. The extractor now selects the member whose sha256 matches the catalog pin (zip stays unpinned transport; decompression caps unchanged). (2) pth conversion failed with 'No module named onnxscript': torch >=2.9 switched torch.onnx.export to the dynamo exporter by default. The export now pins the legacy exporter (dynamo=False) that the dynamic-axes design targets, and the converter image additionally ships onnx+onnxscript so either path works. Both bugs were caught by the sha256/verification gates working as designed - imports refused cleanly instead of installing wrong bytes. Tests: pytest 99 (zip tests rewritten to pinned-member semantics), xUnit 190. All 7 docker images released in lockstep as v1.8.3.9. | v1.8.3.8: Install ANY OpenModelDB model - importer everywhere + pth converter + visible favorites. (1) The AI SERVICE now owns the import pipeline: new endpoints GET /models/import-catalog (public catalog view with per-entry eligibility), POST /models/import-from-catalog (download from allowlisted host, sha256 pin, validated ingest - now also handles ZIP-packed releases like the AnimeJaNai series: the pinned zip is verified, exactly one .onnx extracted with a decompression cap), POST /models/convert-from-catalog and POST /models/convert-upload. The upload/import/convert paths share one hardened core (_ingest_onnx_bytes). (2) NEW OPT-IN CONVERTER IMAGE docker7-converter (CPU image + torch/spandrel, ~2 GB): converts OpenModelDB .pth/.safetensors models to ONNX (opset 17, dynamic axes) and VERIFIES the export against the torch output before registering - unlocks ~500 of the 609 pth-only community models (240 fully automatic via pinned downloads, the rest via file upload). Standard images answer 501 with a clear pointer. The catalog generator now records sha256 pins for convertible models too (the converter refuses unpinned downloads). (3) The :5000 dashboard got an 'Import from OpenModelDB' section (search, license/NC badges, one-click import/convert, API-token field) and custom models are badged in the model table. (4) The plugin config page picker now shows the WHOLE direct catalog (60): ready-to-use ONNX, convertible (when the converter image runs), and manual-install entries greyed out with the reason; plus a NEW file-install form (.onnx installs directly, .pth converts) that closes the gap for Google-Drive/Mega-hosted models - download in the browser, hand the file over, done. Installed files are auto-pinned to favorites. (5) NEW '★ Favorites' management card: every pinned model with status (loaded/ready), Use and Unpin actions. (6) The plugin import/importable endpoints delegate to the service when available (downloads run in the container, not the Jellyfin process) with a transparent fallback for older images. Tests: pytest 99 (16 new gate/zip/converter invariants), xUnit 190, UI-consistency 120 refs, build clean. | v1.8.3.7: Model favorites + import search + restart-proof imports (the '6.1' follow-up to the importer). (1) NEW '★ Favorites' group at the TOP of the model dropdown: pin/unpin the selected model with the new star button next to the dropdown, and every one-click import is pinned automatically - pick a community model, hit Import, and it sits at the top of the list ready to select. Favorites persist in the plugin config (new FavoriteModels field). (2) Search box for the import picker - filters the ~40 importable OpenModelDB models live by name/architecture/license/scale. (3) FIX (service): custom/imported models now SURVIVE CONTAINER RESTARTS - /models/upload used to register the model only in memory, so a restart silently dropped it from the catalog while the file kept sitting in the volume (favorites would have pointed at nothing). The upload now writes a small <name>.custom.json metadata sidecar next to the .onnx and the service re-registers all sidecar models at startup; DELETE /models/upload/{name} removes both. Imported models are labelled 'Imported / Custom' in the dropdown. docker7 images released in lockstep as v1.8.3.7. Tests: pytest 83 (5 new persistence invariants), xUnit 190, UI-consistency 114 refs, build clean. | v1.8.3.6: One-click model importer + service version sync. (1) NEW 'Import Community Model' card on the plugin config page: pick any directly importable OpenModelDB model and the PLUGIN does the rest - downloads the file (https-only from an allowlisted host set: github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com, huggingface.co, Oracle object storage; plain .onnx only; 500 MB cap), verifies the catalog's pinned sha256, and hands it to the AI service's existing validated /models/upload - the model is registered as omdb-<id> and immediately selectable in the model list. No curl, no console. Import is catalog-id based (free-form URLs are impossible by design), NC-licensed models show a non-commercial warning before import, and the external download uses a dedicated HttpClient WITHOUT the X-Api-Token handler so the service secret never reaches third-party hosts. New endpoints: GET /Upscaler/models/importable, POST /Upscaler/models/import (admin-only). (2) The docker7 AI-service images now release in lockstep as v1.8.3.6, so the service dashboard shows the same version as the plugin (it had legitimately stayed at 1.8.3.4 - the images only rebuild on docker tags and no service change had shipped - but mismatched numbers read as a broken update). No functional service change. (3) Catalog check against today's OpenModelDB refresh: no new freely-licensed direct-ONNX candidates since the 2026-07 model eval (every 2025/2026 addition is CC-BY-NC) - exactly the gap the importer now covers for private use. Tests: xUnit 186 (22 new importer-gate invariants), pytest 78, build clean. | v1.8.3.5: Hardening + importable-models catalog. (1) Release guard now asserts ALL THREE plugin feeds (manifest.json + repository-jellyfin.json + repository-simple.json) carry the release with a consistent checksum and correct sourceUrl, live AND in the local checkout - kills the issue-#74 class where the most-advertised feed silently lagged behind. (2) New CI check (Scripts/check_ui_field_consistency.py): every element id referenced from JS must exist in the HTML - kills the v1.8.3.3 class where config-page JS referenced a removed input and crashed saving at runtime despite a clean build; also runs inside verify-release. It immediately found a real one: the test-upscale button read the non-existent #PreferredModel and silently always tested realesrgan-x4 - it now tests the actually selected #Model. (3) UserManagerAdapter caches the GetUserData MethodInfo (previously resolved per user x item on every library scan). (4) 5 new pytest invariants for the face-restore helpers (_feathered_mask shape/center/feathering/range, restore_faces_in_frame no-model no-op). (5) NEW site page 'Importable models': 60 ready-to-use ONNX + 609 convertible community models from OpenModelDB with license badges (NC flagged), sizes, sha256 pins and direct download links; data refreshed weekly by the new import-catalog-refresh CI workflow, page renders models-import.json client-side (single data source). The site AI assistant now answers questions about these models (instant card for a specific model name, catalog context otherwise) and explains the import flow (manual today per docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md, one-click importer planned for v1.9). Note: the planned recommendation-endpoint consolidation was intentionally NOT done - /recommend, /recommendations and /recommend-model are three different functions consumed by different UI surfaces, not aliases; documented in-code instead. Tests: pytest 78, xUnit 164, build clean. | v1.8.3.4: Jellyfin 12.0 readiness + model-catalog integrity pass. (1) Jellyfin 12.0 RC: a static build against Jellyfin.Controller 12.0.0-rc2 surfaced exactly ONE break — 12.0 replaces the IUserManager.Users property with GetUsers(); UserManagerAdapter now resolves user enumeration and GetUserData via reflection, so one DLL serves 10.11.x AND 12.x (0 compile errors against both SDKs). The embedded web JS already uses only the modern 'Authorization: MediaBrowser' scheme, so 12.0's default rejection of legacy auth does not affect the plugin. Break list + RC runtime test plan: docs/JELLYFIN-12-READINESS.md. (2) Catalog integrity: HEAD-checked all 73 model download URLs — 16 were dead (8 HuggingFace repos deleted upstream). 9 entries repointed to verified mirrors (real-cugan x2/x4 op18 exports, omnisr-x2 HFA2k, omnisr-x4 official weights, drct-l-x4 RealWebPhoto DRCT-L export, gpen-512 + restoreformer-plus-plus via the FaceFusion assets mirror, nafnet SIDD-width64 export, rife-v4.25 op21-slim) and 7 marked [self-host required] (craft x2/x4, bhi-realplksr-x4, dat-light x2/x4, man x2/x4 — no public ONNX exists anywhere). (3) Three license-checked model additions, sha256-pinned and CPU-benchmarked: purephoto-realplksr-x4 (CC-BY-SA-4.0, photo/portrait), nomos8kdat-x4 (CC-BY-4.0, JPEG restoration for old rips), fallin-soft-x2 (CC-BY-4.0, real-time 1080p anime); clearreality-x4 repinned to the author's Apache-2.0 opset17 build (measured 32x faster than realesrgan-x4 on CPU: 4ms vs 129ms per 64px tile); ultrasharp-v2-x4 now carries its CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 flag (was unlabelled). New catalog fields: license, attribution, sha256 — and download_model now VERIFIES the sha256 before a downloaded model is activated (supply-chain guard). Rejected candidates + rationale: docs/MODEL-EVAL-2026-07.md. (4) Docker supply-chain: explicit starlette>=1.0.1 floor (BadHost auth-bypass CVE-2026-48710) plus Pillow>=12.2.0, python-multipart>=0.0.30, opencv-contrib-python>=4.12.0 across all 7 requirement sets; base images re-pinned (python:3.12-slim 2026-06-24 digest x3, CUDA 12.8.0->12.8.1); docker-publish now also updates :latest, which had been stuck on a v1.7.8-era image. Tests: xUnit 164, dotnet build clean. | v1.8.3.3: Fix config-page Save (regression in v1.8.3.2). The v1.8.3.2 SSH cleanup left one stale, unguarded line in the settings Save handler (config.RemoteUser = page.querySelector('#RemoteUser').value) that referenced the removed input and threw a TypeError, so saving plugin settings failed. Removed that line; no other change. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean. | v1.8.3.2: Remove dead SSH remote-transcoding (Issue #73). The docker7 image dropped sshd long ago (non-root appuser hardening), but the plugin still shipped SSH remote-transcoding config + UI, a /Upscaler/ssh/test endpoint that shelled out to ssh, and an FFmpeg-wrapper-script installer (/Upscaler/wrapper/*). All removed: 8 SSH config fields, the ssh/test + wrapper endpoints, FFmpegWrapperService + FFmpegWrapperController + the now-orphaned PlatformDetectionService, and the config-page sections. Offloading to a GPU host now uses the existing HTTP path only (set AI Service URL to http://host:5000, no SSH). Security: removes an admin endpoint that executed ssh and an on-disk script installer. Plugin-only; existing docker7 images unaffected. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean. | v1.8.3.1: Cancel-fix (plugin-only). jobs/{id}/cancel reported success but never stopped a running job: VideoProcessor.ProcessVideoAsync created a per-job CancellationTokenSource (the one CancelJob cancels) but passed the bare caller token to the processing pipeline instead of the job's linked cts.Token, so cancellation never reached extraction/upscaling. The semaphore wait, model-load and the method executor now all receive cts.Token (still linked to the caller token), so Cancel actually stops the job promptly. Pre-existing across all processing methods. Tests: xUnit 164, build clean. | v1.8.3: Opt-in pipeline parallelism. New experimental setting 'Pipeline parallelism' (default OFF) overlaps frame extraction with upscaling instead of running them strictly back-to-back: while ffmpeg is still extracting later frames, already-extracted frames are upscaled concurrently. Built on a thread-safe FrameStreamCoordinator that hands a frame to the upscaler only once a successor frame proves it was fully written, and drops the unproven highest frame if extraction fails, so output frame-count and ordering are identical to the sequential path. Default behaviour is unchanged; the overlap runs only when the box is ticked. Plugin-only: works with the existing docker7 AI-service images, no rebuild needed. Tests: FrameStreamCoordinator state-machine (COMPLETE-vs-FAILED asymmetry, terminal-first-wins), xUnit 164, dotnet build clean. | v1.8.2: Quality + hardening pass. (1) Denoise-before-encode prefilter (hqdn3d / nlmeans) — a dedicated source-clean pass run BEFORE upscaling/encoding (the 'Netflix lesson': clean input -> better SR + smaller output), independent of the camera-style filters; config exposes Enable Denoise + engine + strength. (2) VMAF quality scoring — new admin-only POST /Upscaler/vmaf compares an upscaled/distorted file to a reference via ffmpeg+libvmaf, returning the pooled 0-100 score (501 if this ffmpeg build lacks libvmaf). (3) MultiFrame extraction now reports live progress — the frame-count poller previously only covered FrameByFrame/Batch; the MultiFrame path extracted inline with no %. (4) Second frame-interpolation architecture beside RIFE: the interpolation engine is now input-signature-adaptive (RIFE 1/2-input, IFRNet 3-input incl. timestep, CAIN 2x3ch no-timestep); IFRNet + CAIN added to the catalog as experimental self-host (clear 501 instead of a 404 auto-download). (5) Decoupled model download: POST /models/download-async + GET /models/download-status/{id} run big downloads in the background so they no longer trip client/proxy timeouts. (6) Supply-chain: all 6 Dockerfile base images pinned by @sha256 digest + a pip-audit CVE sweep across the requirement sets (0 known vulnerabilities). (7) New client-VSR-vs-server doc chapter, FrameStreamCoordinator (verified extraction-vs-upscale ordering core, staged behind the default path), and added tests: hardware detection, denoise prefilter, VMAF helpers, interpolation dispatch, async download, multi-frame VSR contract, stream coordinator. | v1.8.1: Live filter fix + hardware-aware model recommendation. (1) The in-player live filters (presets/sliders) had no visible effect while realtime client-side upscaling was active — the upscaler renders to a canvas overlay that covered the filtered <video>, so the CSS filter sat on a hidden element; it is now also applied to the visible canvas. (2) New hardware-aware recommendation (service GET /recommend + plugin GET /Upscaler/recommend) picks a model+scale the detected hardware can actually run (weak CPU -> fsrcnn-x2 @2x, dedicated GPU -> realesrgan-x4) to avoid the wrong-model-on-weak-hardware wall. Tests: pytest + xUnit + dotnet build. | v1.8.0: Managed API tokens. The new 'API Tokens' tab (Jellyfin admin) mints named tokens for the AI service with an optional expiry (30 days / 90 days / 1 year / never); each is shown once and stored only as a SHA-256 hash, and can be revoked individually. The service keeps them in a persistent /app/config volume (new ai-config mount) and authenticates X-Api-Token against the env API_TOKEN (now a non-revocable bootstrap) OR any active managed token - both timing-safe, expiry checked lazily (no background sweep). New endpoints: service /auth/tokens (GET/POST/DELETE), plugin /Upscaler/tokens (admin-only proxy). NOTE: needs the AI service image >= v1.8.0 (rebuild via docker-ai-service/build-and-push.ps1) so /auth/tokens exists. | v1.7.13: UI redesign + API-token docs. The plugin config page now adopts the Operator-Console design of the Docker service UI (consistent dark theme, cards, KPI tiles, chips, blue accent) - CSS-only re-theme, all settings/JS unchanged. Added a 'Securing the AI service (API token)' section: API_TOKEN is a container env var (set in compose/run, match it in the plugin field; API_TOKEN=disable for a trusted LAN). | v1.7.12: Timeout-wall + error-key fixes (plugin-only). (1) First-time model loads that auto-download large models (face-restore GFPGAN/CodeFormer/GPEN ~280-377MB, large ONNX) no longer hit the 120s proxy wall: a dedicated 570s download client - kept just under the 600s UI timeout so an over-long download surfaces a readable error instead of a body-less browser timeout; benchmarks moved 120s->300s; the /models/load UI timeout was raised 180s->600s to match. (2) The UI error parsers now read detail||error||message, so the real reason (the controller 'error' key) is shown instead of a generic 'Load failed'. New DI test asserts the named-client timeouts so a typo cannot silently fall back to the 100s default. | v1.7.11: Honest progress + version-display guard. (1) The 'job stuck at 95%' fix now covers the frame-EXTRACTION phase: batch progress shows real extraction + upscale progress (frames done/total) instead of a time estimate that pinned at 95% during the long ffmpeg extraction on slow hardware (#72); the dashboard panel shows the live phase (Extracting/Upscaling/Encoding) instead of 'Idle'. (2) Fixed the pipe-encode path that could also pin at 95% (unknown-total sentinel). (3) configurationpage.html now shows the real plugin version (v1.7.10 shipped still rendering v1.7.9), and a release guard asserts every user-visible version string matches the tag so it cannot drift again. Plugin-only (no Docker functional change). | v1.7.10: Fixes the recurring 'job stuck at 95%' + sharper GPU diagnostics. (1) Batch progress now reflects REAL frames processed (frames done/total + fps + ETA) instead of a time estimate that pinned at 95% on slow hardware; and a job with NO model loaded now FAILS FAST with a clear 'load a model first' message instead of grinding to 95% and hanging (#70/#72). (2) Setup Doctor now detects 'GPU + CUDA/ROCm available but inference on CPU' = a host driver/toolkit mismatch (right fix: update the host driver / check logs) instead of mislabeling it a CPU image (#71). (3) Plugin's displayed version (player/quick-menu/sidebar) synced to the real release. Docker + plugin both 1.7.10. | v1.7.9: Setup Doctor — new GET /doctor self-diagnostic (backend, GPU passthrough, onnxruntime build, API token, model smoke) + a 'Setup Check' panel in the Docker dashboard, condensing the #66/#69/#70 setup-friction saga into one call. Fixed three AI-service endpoints (run_benchmark, /models/load, /benchmark-frame) that still reported the *requested* GPU intent instead of the *active* provider truth — now all use gpu_is_active(). Anime4K anime-shader realtime tier now ships a VENDORED, tree-shaken Anime4K.js (v1.1.2, WebGL, MIT, ~225KB) embedded in the plugin DLL instead of a dead CDN load, with a WebGL2 float-texture support-gate and automatic Lanczos fallback; honest tier label (anime shader, not a neural net). Docker AI service and plugin both at 1.7.9. | v1.6.1.16: Hotfix for issue #64 — LibraryUpscaleScanTask was failing on every item with two related cold-start errors: first "File not found: ffprobe", then after the partial VideoAnalyzer fix a second-stage "Cannot start process because a file name has not been provided" from VideoFrameProcessor.ExtractFramesAsync. Root cause: THREE singletons (VideoAnalyzer, VideoFrameProcessor, ProcessingMethodExecutor) each captured an empty ffmpeg/ffprobe path string at construction time, before Jellyfin's MediaEncoder had finished resolving /usr/lib/jellyfin-ffmpeg/{ffmpeg,ffprobe}. Fix: new EnsureFFmpegReady() in VideoProcessor late-resolves EncoderPath/ProbePath from MediaEncoder on demand, reconfigures FFMpegCore's GlobalFFOptions, and propagates the fresh paths into all three sub-services via new UpdateFFprobePath() and UpdateFFmpegPath() methods. Called at construction + again at the top of ProcessVideoAsync / ExtractSingleFrameAsync / ExtractSingleFrameWithFiltersAsync, so cold-start scheduled tasks work even when plugin boots before MediaEncoder. Verified live on Jellyfin 10.11.8: scan task now progresses past frame extraction where previously 70/70 items failed instantly. README header synced 1.6.1.14 → 1.6.1.16 (was missed in v1.6.1.15 release). | v1.6.1.15: Docker AI service operator console redesigned from the ground up — replaces the AI-aesthetic gradient/neon look with a professional operator dashboard inspired by Grafana/Linear/Vercel. New layout: sticky topbar with live connection dot + auth posture chip, 4 tabs (Overview/Models/Hardware/API), KPI tiles with tabular-num typography, inline SVG latency sparkline (rolling 60 samples, refreshed from /metrics every 5s), feature table wired to /features endpoint, model catalog table driven by /models with search + status filter (all/available/downloaded/loaded/unavailable), hardware panel sourced from /hardware + /gpus with per-provider chips, and an API browser that pulls /openapi.json and renders every endpoint with method+path+tags. Zero placeholders — every widget hits a real endpoint or surfaces a transparent error state. CSS uses system fonts + subtle zinc/slate palette, no gradients, no emojis, monospace for numbers. Library-Scan codec picker expanded from 3 options to 12: software (libx264/libx265/libsvtav1/libaom-av1/libvpx-vp9), NVIDIA NVENC (h264/hevc/av1_nvenc), Intel QSV (h264/hevc/av1_qsv), plus Stream Copy — with per-codec encoder defaults (libsvtav1 -preset 6 -crf 30, libaom-av1 -cpu-used 4 -crf 30, libvpx-vp9 -crf 31 -row-mt 1, NVENC -cq per H/HEVC/AV1, QSV global_quality) instead of one-size-fits-all -crf 23. Player FPS HUD (the "AI Nfps | WebGL" top-left badge) now auto-hides in sync with Jellyfin's OSD fade: 200ms poll mirrors .videoOsdBottom opacity, 180ms transition → HUD only visible while playback controls are visible. FFmpeg wrapper install path documented: POST /Upscaler/wrapper/install generates upscale-wrapper.sh (Unix) or upscale-wrapper.bat + upscale-logic.ps1 (Windows) next to the plugin DLL; remote-SSH logic preserved for EnableRemoteTranscoding. Docker VERSION bumped 1.6.1.13 → 1.6.1.15 (1.6.1.14 never shipped as image). | v1.6.1.14: Select Library feature (issue #64) — scheduled library scan can now target specific libraries instead of always scanning everything. New UI: chip-based library picker on Settings tab with individual checkboxes per library, persisted via new plugin config field EnabledLibraryIds (comma-separated Jellyfin virtual-folder IDs). Empty = legacy behavior (scan all, unchanged). Backend: new GET /Upscaler/libraries endpoint enumerates virtual folders with ID/name/collectionType/paths; LibraryUpscaleScanTask resolves enabled IDs to physical paths on scan start and filters fetched items against those paths before processing. Bugfixes: (1) saveConfig in configurationpage.html now actually writes EnabledLibraryIds (was added to load fields but missing from save payload, so chip selection silently reset on every save). (2) config.PluginVersion hardcoded string + header meta version synced 1.6.1.13→1.6.1.14 (previously save always wrote old version back). (3) sidebar-upscaler.js PLUGIN_VERSION const synced 1.6.1.13→1.6.1.14. (4) csproj Version/AssemblyVersion/FileVersion synced so Jellyfin plugin manager shows the actual release version instead of one-version-behind. Docker AI service UI redesign: API_TOKEN not-configured state now shows a single persistent dismissible banner instead of repeat-spamming toasts on every /status poll; toast dedup window (10s) + max-3 stack + click-to-dismiss; new model search + status filter on the AI Models card. FastAPI /status returns api_token_configured and auth_enabled so the UI can distinguish "auth disabled on purpose" from "forgot to set token". | v1.6.1.13: In-player quick-menu redesigned + live filter controls. The overlay that pops up from the Upscaler button during playback is now a tabbed interface [Models | Filters | Realtime] instead of a flat scroll. New Filters tab lets you tune the video look WITHOUT leaving playback: pick any of 15 preset looks (Cinematic, Vintage, Vivid, Sepia, Cyberpunk, Teal&Orange, ...) or drag 3 live sliders (Brightness / Contrast / Saturation) — the <video> element's CSS filter property updates at ~60fps for instant feedback, no re-transcode. An Advanced collapsible exposes server-side params (Gamma, Sharpness, Color Temperature, Vignette, Film Grain, Denoise) that persist via new POST /Upscaler/filter-config endpoint and kick in via FFmpeg on next seek. Reset/Save on both tiers. LIVE indicator dot pulses green while the CSS overlay is applied. Live sliders work fully offline — no AI service required; only the advanced FFmpeg params need the filter chain path. | v1.6.1.12: Bugfix + model-catalog cleanup. (1) gfpgan-v1.4 + codeformer URLs repointed from the never-published kuscheltier/jellyfin-vsr-models repo to the public facefusion/models-3.0.0 mirror; both flipped to available:True. (2) RIFE frame-interpolation replaced: old rife-v4.6 / rife-v4.6-lite pointed at a 404 v0.0.0 placeholder tag, now replaced with rife-v4.9 (quality) / rife-v4.8 (balanced, new) / rife-v4.7 (fast) — all from the yuvraj108c/rife-onnx ONNX export. MODEL_ALIASES map ensures saved configs referencing the old rife-v4.6 / rife-v4.6-lite keys keep working transparently. (3) Five models without public ONNX mirrors (edvr-m-x4, realbasicvsr-x4, animesr-v2-x4, apisr-x3, nomos8k-hat-x4) now carry a [self-host required] name suffix and link to new docs/MODEL-HOSTING.md — self-host recipe for PyTorch→ONNX export. (4) New Scripts/verify-release.ps1 runs end-to-end release validation after gh release create: downloads assets, re-computes SHA against live manifest checksum, inspects ZIP contents for forbidden patterns (Moq/Mono.Cecil/InstrumentationEngine/CodeCoverage/Scripts/runtimes/.pdb/.deps.json) that indicated the v1.6.1.11 wrong-ZIP-upload bug. Exit 1 on any mismatch. (5) docker service VERSION + Dockerfile LABELs + all csproj/HTML/JS version strings synced to 1.6.1.12. Net catalog: 32 models available (up from 30), 5 self-host documented (down from 12 broken). | v1.6.1.11: Bugfix — (1) Regex widened to ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)*$ so model IDs with dots (rife-v4.6, gfpgan-v1.4, rife-v4.6-lite) no longer return 400; path-traversal patterns (.., leading/trailing dots) still blocked. (2) Docker manifest: 9 broken/pending model entries (nomos8k-hat-x4, apisr-x3, edvr-m-x4, realbasicvsr-x4, animesr-v2-x4, rife-v4.6, rife-v4.6-lite, gfpgan-v1.4, codeformer) flipped to available:False so the UI filters them out instead of fast-failing on load. (3) Fixed 3 more raw fetch() calls in player-integration.js that were silently 401-ing against Upscaler/models/load, Upscaler/upscale-frame and Upscaler/benchmark-frame — same root-cause as v1.6.1.10 (ApiClient.getRequestHeader does not exist; switched to ApiClient.accessToken() bearer header). (4) GET /Upscaler/jobs now requires RequiresElevation — previously leaked active-job file paths to any authenticated user. (5) Jellyfin.Controller SDK pin bumped 10.11.6 → 10.11.8 to match targetAbi (closes patch-level API surface mismatch). (6) Docker service VERSION string + Dockerfile LABELs synced to 1.6.1.11. Bulk-load test: 30 of 30 available models load successfully. | v1.6.1.10: Hotfix — quick-menu model states (download/loaded flags) now load correctly. Raw fetch() to /Upscaler/models was returning 401 because ApiClient.getRequestHeader() doesn't exist in current apiclient builds, so no auth header was attached. Switched _fetchModelStates to ApiClient.ajax which injects X-Emby-Token automatically. Menu summary now shows accurate N of 42 models ready counter. | v1.6.1.9: Redesigned player quick-menu — glassmorphic 380px layout, custom on/off switch, filter chips (All/Downloaded/Recommended), live summary strip (X of Y models ready), per-model download-state icons (ready/download/busy/error) with spinner shown in-place during load. Menu stays open during model loads so users can see progress. Smarter failure messaging: surfaces config-specific hints when AI service auth fails. NEW: AiServiceApiToken plugin config + X-Api-Token auth handler auto-injects the shared secret on every AI service call (fixes 403 API_TOKEN errors on /models/download and /models/load). | v1.6.1.8: Quick-menu model picker now actually loads the model — calls POST /Upscaler/models/load to make the AI service map the weights into memory (previously only persisted the config without warming the service). Error toasts now show the real HTTP/service error instead of a generic failure message. Menu dismisses immediately so the load-progress toast is visible. | v1.6.1.7: AI Face Restoration — GFPGAN v1.4 + CodeFormer integration with OpenCV Haar face detection, feathered alpha-blend paste-back, configurable max-faces & resolution guard. 8 new camera-style filter presets (Sepia, Pastel, Cyberpunk, Drama, Soft Glow, Sharp HD, Retro Game, Teal & Orange). Live Filter Preview now auto-updates on preset dropdown change (300ms debounce, supersedes in-flight requests). Diagnostic docs for the 9 multi-frame / interpolation / Vulkan models that fail single-frame bench. Docker service version synced to 1.6.1.7 with /face-restore/* endpoints. | v1.6.1.6: Live Filter Preview on Filters tab — pick a video, apply any preset to a real frame, compare before/after. New endpoint GET /Upscaler/filter-preview/frame/{itemId}?preset=X. | v1.6.1.5: Fix SMB/network share support in Comparison View — use Jellyfin IMediaEncoder for path resolution. | v1.6.1.4: Comparison View now extracts real video frames via FFmpeg instead of poster images. | v1.6.1.3: Fix controller route — remove api/ prefix so frontend API calls reach the controller. | v1.6.1.2: Fix remaining red Error states on Test Connection/Upscale/SSH buttons. | v1.6.1.1: Fix misleading Error/Unavailable states — show Standalone mode when AI service not configured. Neutral gray styling. Add local filter benchmark. Version display fix. Save button on Filters tab. | v1.6.1.0: Camera-style video filters (7 presets + custom mode). Filter-preview API endpoint.
